data.title: Ryan Specialty signals $80M annual savings from Empower restructuring as property pressures persist
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ryan Specialty Holdings (RYAN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Patrick Ryan, Founder & Executive Chairman, stated that “for the quarter, we delivered organic growth of 6.6%” and highlighted surpassing “revenues of $3 billion, up 21% year-over-year, driven by organic growth of 10.1%, on top of 12.8% in 2024, and significant contributions from our M&A strategy.” He noted, “we completed 5 acquisitions with trailing revenue of over $125 million” and emphasized a deliberate decision to diversify with “delegated authority, including both binding authority, and underwriting management.” Ryan announced the launch of Empower, a 3-year restructuring program, anticipating “a cumulative special charge of approximately $160 million through 2028” and expecting “the program will deliver approximately $80 million of annual savings in 2029.”

  • Ryan also shared that the board authorized a $300 million share repurchase program, saying, “this decision reflects our view that there’s a meaningful dislocation between our current valuation and our confidence in the near and long-term outlook of our business.”

  • CEO Timothy Turner described 2025 as “the most challenging property environment the insurance industry has faced in decades,” reporting that “the fourth quarter was particularly challenging. We saw a further decline in property pricing as the quarter progressed…it was most notable in the month of December, particularly on certain large accounts where pricing was down 25% to 35%.” He detailed continued optimism in high hazard lines and noted that “our delegated authority business manages north of $10 billion in premium across more than 300 products.”

  • CFO Janice Hamilton reported, “In Q4, total revenue grew 13% period-over-period to $751 million,” with “organic revenue growth of 6.6%” and M&A adding “over 5 percentage points to our top line.” She shared, “Adjusted EBITDAC grew 2.9% to $222 million. Adjusted EBITDAC margin was 29.6% compared to 32.6% in the prior year period. Adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.45 was comparable period-over-period.” Hamilton confirmed an 8% increase to the regular quarterly dividend for Class A stockholders and reiterated the $300 million share repurchase program.

Outlook

  • The company guides to organic revenue growth “in the high single digits for 2026,” with Hamilton stating, “this reflects our current view of market conditions, including continued property pricing pressures, a more moderate pace of casualty growth and broader macroeconomic uncertainty.” She noted, “for the full year 2026, we are guiding to an adjusted EBITDAC margin of flat to moderately down as compared to the prior year.” Hamilton also projected, “we expect the program will deliver approximately $80 million of annual savings in 2029.”

Financial Results

  • For Q4 2025, total revenue was $751 million, with organic revenue growth of 6.6%. Adjusted EBITDAC reached $222 million, and the adjusted EBITDAC margin was 29.6%. Adjusted diluted earnings per share stood at $0.45. For the full year, total revenue surpassed $3 billion, adjusted EBITDAC was $967 million, and adjusted EPS was $1.96. The company ended the quarter at 3.2x total net leverage.

  • M&A contributed to revenue growth, with five acquisitions adding over $125 million in trailing revenue. The board approved both an 8% dividend increase and a $300 million share repurchase program.

Q&A

  • Elyse Greenspan, Wells Fargo: Asked about the reduction from double-digit organic growth guidance to high single digits, especially given property price declines. Hamilton explained, “the fourth quarter really marked an intensification of some of these property pricing trends. We saw particularly in the large accounts, rate decreases to 25% to 35%, which was higher than what we were seeing earlier in the year…that strong growth that we experienced in 2025, we expect to moderate within 2026.” Turner added, “we believe there's plenty of new business for us to capture this year, and we continue to look for new innovative ways to broker that business and underwrite it.”

  • Greenspan also raised concerns about AI’s impact on brokers. Ryan responded, “we look at AI as an ally, not as an adversary. Lots of opportunities for us to embrace AI and improve…we also believe that there's going to be some significant efficiencies through AI. We can't quantify them at this time.”

  • Taylor Scott, Barclays: Inquired about construction market headwinds and the share repurchase. Turner clarified, “the construction segment and practice group for us remains very, very strong…macro-economic pressure and the interest rates have slowed down the timeline between submit to quote to bind.” Hamilton added, “just given the continued uncertainty from a macroeconomic perspective, it is still early…to tell…how that will play out in '26.”

  • Brian Meredith, UBS: Asked about client demand and margin outlook. Turner stated, “we don't see any measurable trends of buying less…structure demands are a little bit different. So higher retention levels in certain accounts, alternative risk…comes into play.” Hamilton noted, “high single digits. We are pleased with that expectation for '26. We believe that, that will be industry-leading growth.”

  • Meyer Shields, KBW: Questioned the deferred 35% margin target. Hamilton answered, “we deferred the goal of the 35% margin target beyond 2027…Project Empower is intended to support the efficiencies…to contribute to that modest margin expansion in most years. But at this point, we're not putting a time line around it.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on growth guidance, margin outlook, and AI-related risks, reflecting a neutral to slightly negative tone as they probed for risks and sustainability of performance.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, but during Q&A, explanations became more measured, particularly when discussing headwinds in property and margin expansion timelines. Phrases such as “we believe…” and “we expect…” indicated a cautious approach.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more cautious regarding growth rates and margin expansion, especially as the company shifted from double-digit to high single-digit growth guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from double-digit organic growth in the previous quarter to high single digits for 2026, reflecting intensified property price declines and moderating casualty growth.

  • Margin guidance remains “flat to moderately down,” continuing the trend of near-term margin pressure due to talent and technology investments; the 35% margin target remains deferred.

  • The previous quarter focused on aggressive talent acquisition and M&A; this quarter announced the Empower restructuring program to drive efficiency and future savings.

  • Analyst questions in both quarters focused on growth sustainability, margin outlook, and impacts of industry trends such as AI and market competition, but concerns about property headwinds and pace of margin improvement were more prominent this quarter.

  • Management’s optimism about growth opportunities and diversification was more tempered this quarter in response to ongoing market volatility and competitive pressures.

Risks and Concerns

  • Intensified property pricing declines, especially in large accounts, with rate decreases of 25% to 35% in December.

  • Ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty impacting large construction projects and overall business mix.

  • Margin pressures from recent talent and technology investments, with Empower aimed at mitigating long-term cost growth.

  • Analyst concerns about the potential for AI-driven disruption and the sustainability of profit commissions in a softening market.

Final Takeaway

Ryan Specialty Holdings delivered strong full-year results despite significant headwinds, with leadership emphasizing diversification, continued investment in talent and technology, and the launch of the Empower program to drive future efficiency and margin improvements. The company guides to high single-digit organic growth for 2026 and anticipates $80 million in annual savings from Empower starting in 2029, while maintaining flexibility in capital allocation through M&A and the newly authorized share repurchase program. Management remains committed to industry-leading growth, though near-term momentum is expected to moderate amid ongoing property market pressures and macroeconomic uncertainty.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ryan Specialty Group raises dividend by 8.3% to $0.13
data.description:
• Ryan Specialty Group (RYAN) declares $0.13/share quarterly dividend, 8.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.12.
  • Forward yield 1.17%
  • Payable March 10; for shareholders of record Feb. 24; ex-div Feb. 24.
  •  A portion of the dividend, $0.06 per share, will be funded by free cash flow from Ryan Specialty, LLC and will be paid to all holders of the Company’s Class A common stock and the holders of the LLC Common Units
  • See RYAN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: L'Oreal Non-GAAP EPS of €11.44, revenue of €44.05B
data.description:
• L'Oreal press release (LRLCF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €11.44.
  • Revenue of €44.05B (+1.3% Y/Y).
  • Gross margin up +10 basis points at 74.3%.
  • Operating margin up +20 basis points at 20.2%.
  • Net cash flow: 7.2 billion euros, up +7.8%.
